title: Navasan.tech Daily OHLC Backfill -- USD (usd_sell), Bahar Azadi coin, Emami coin
time_coverage: 1405-03-01 to 1405-04-23 (Persian/Jalali) = approx. 2026-05-22 to 2026-07-14 Gregorian; 49 daily OHLC rows for usd_sell, 54 for bahar and sekkeh
units: IMPORTANT -- units differ from the /latest/ endpoint for the SAME field names. In ohlcSearch, 'bahar' and 'sekkeh' are already in PLAIN TOMAN (multiply by 10 for rial), NOT thousands-of-toman like in /latest/. Confirmed: ohlc close for bahar on 1405-04-23 = 175,000,000 toman = 1,750,000,000 rial, matching the /latest/ snapshot's x10,000-scaled value (1,750,000,000) from the same day -- i.e. ohlcSearch's raw number already equals /latest/'s raw number x1000. Do not apply the same multiplier convention across both endpoints without checking. usd_sell values are plain toman as expected (x10 for rial).
license_terms: Commercial API, free tier ~120 requests/month; data used here for corroboration/citation only, not redistribution of the raw feed.
notes: Requested via item=usd_sell/bahar/sekkeh with Persian-calendar start/end params (Gregorian YYYY-MM-DD returned an empty [] result for the same item/range -- ohlcSearch requires Jalali dates, unlike the docs' generic 'start date, end date' wording). 3 calls, well within the ~120/month free-tier budget (usage endpoint showed monthly_usage=2 even after these calls, i.e. ohlcSearch calls may not count against the same counter as /latest/, or the counter lags -- did not rely on this, stopped well under budget regardless). This backfill cross-validates against this project's existing TGJU-sourced daily series in fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn (parallel USD) and the two gold-coin charts for the overlapping window (through 2026-07-11); it also extends 3 days past the existing charts' last point (2026-07-11) through 2026-07-14.

failures.reason: ohlcSearch appears to require Jalali/Persian calendar dates, not Gregorian; retried successfully with item=usd_sell and Persian dates
